metadata_content.caption:
This drink is so simple but really hits the spot🌸 Orange Diet Coke Cold Brew for the win 🍊✨
Try it and tell me in the comments what you think🫶🏻

Ingredients:
🍊1 orange 
🍊6tbsps Ground coffee beans 
🍊1/2cup water 
🍊1/4th cup freshly squeezed orange juice
🍊Chilled Diet Coke

Process
For the cold brew:
•Wash and slice the orange into thick slices
•Add the slices into a jar and muddle them slightly, add the instant coffee and water to it and give it a nice stir
•Cover and let this cold brew proof for 12 to 24hours in the fridge. Strain the brew with the help of a cloth or strainer.

Assembly:
•In a glass add lots of ice cube and orange juice.
•Pour over 1/2cup of prepared cold brew and top it up with a chilled Diet Coke
•Add a slice of orange and enjoy it

metadata_content.caption:
These are the only 5 tips you need to make the (best) Fluffiest Bhaturaaaaaah 🤤🔥! 
. 
✨Tip 1 - Use Chiroti Rava - It hydrates fast, makes the dough lighter & helps the bhatura puff evenly every single time. 
✨Tip 2 - Use Very Sour Yogurt - The acidity helps the dough ferment better, which means softer centres and a fuller puff when it hits the hot oil.
✨Tip 3 - Knead for 8 to 10 Minutes - This builds stretchiness in the dough so it turns smooth, elastic. 
✨Tip 4 - Oil Temperature - The oil needs to be hot enough for the bhatura to puff instantly and cook evenly, without turning dark too fast.
✨Tip 5 - Pair It Right - A bhatura is only truly a bhaturaaaah when you pair it with an ice cold Coke. Non negotiable! 
.
Fluffiest Bhatura 
. 
Maida - 250g 
Chiroti Rava - 50g (to be soaked with 50ml water) 
Baking soda - 1/2 tsp 
Salt - 1 1/2 tsp 
Sugar - 1 1/2 tsp 
Sour yogurt - 100g 
Water - 45 ml (extra to be used whilst kneading the dough) 
Oil - 1 tbsp 
Oil, for frying 
.
1. First soak the Chiroti Rava in 50 ml water and set it aside for 10 minutes so it softens.
2. In a large bowl add Maida, Baking soda, Salt, Sugar, and the soaked Chiroti Rava. Mix well to combine.
3. Add the Sour yogurt and start bringing the dough together. Add Water (45 ml) little by little as you knead.
4. Drizzle 1 tbsp Oil and continue kneading for 5 to 10 minutes until the dough becomes smooth and stretchy.
5. The dough will feel slacky and very soft. That is exactly what you want for the fluffiest bhaturas, so don’t worry if it feels loose.
6. Cover the bowl and let the dough ferment for 2 to 6 hours in a warm spot. Fermentation time will depend on how warm your kitchen is. The dough should feel light, airy, and slightly risen.
7. Once fermented, shape and fry the bhaturas in hot oil until they puff up evenly.
8. Drain the fried bhaturas on a paper towel lined tray & serve with chole & a chilled Coke! 

#cocacolaindia #realmagic #diljitdosanjh #aaahmeal #harmealaaah #justaddcoke
